issues.title: Event Loop Leakage in AI Provider Singleton
issues.symptom: RuntimeError: Event loop is closed when calling AI services from Celery workers after API requests.
issues.root_cause: GoogleAIProvider was cached with @lru_cache and held an httpx.AsyncClient bound to the first event loop.
issues.resolution: Removed @lru_cache from build_ai_provider() and made GoogleAIProvider loop-aware to reinitialize client on loop mismatch.
issues.lesson_learned: Do not cache stateful async clients globally in hybrid Sync/Async systems.

issues.title: Duplicate Celery Beat Execution in Docker Cluster
issues.symptom: Periodic tasks (for example cleanup_old_chat_sessions_task) ran multiple times simultaneously.
issues.root_cause: Beat (-B) was enabled on all worker nodes, so each replica ran its own scheduler.
issues.resolution: Updated entrypoint-worker.sh to enable Beat only when HOSTNAME ends with -0.
issues.lesson_learned: Celery Beat must run as a singleton in distributed deployments.

issues.title: Redis Global Initialization at Import Time
issues.root_cause: redis_client = get_redis_client() executed at module import in app/core/redis.py before FastAPI loop existed.
issues.resolution: Removed global redis_client and switched code to get_redis_client() / Depends injection.
issues.lesson_learned: Avoid side-effectful infrastructure initialization at module top-level.

issues.title: Memory Task Logic Mismatch
issues.root_cause: Task signature was outdated and called missing method extract_memories_async.
issues.resolution: Updated task signature to (user_id, user_message, assistant_response) and used extract_memories_from_turn.

issues.title: SQLAlchemy Reserved Attribute Crash During Startup
issues.symptom: api and workers restart loops with InvalidRequestError: Attribute name 'metadata' is reserved.
issues.root_cause: DocumentSection declared ORM attribute metadata, conflicting with Declarative reserved name.
issues.resolution: Renamed ORM attribute to artifact_metadata and kept DB column name via mapped_column(name="metadata").
issues.lesson_learned: Never use reserved SQLAlchemy names as mapped attributes; alias at app layer.

issues.title: API Startup Crash From RateLimiter Construction
issues.symptom: API restart loop with ValueError: redis_client is required for RateLimiter.
issues.root_cause: analytics/memories/tree routes instantiated RateLimiter() at import time without Redis client.
issues.resolution: Replaced module-level RateLimiter() with dependency injection: Depends(get_rate_limiter).
issues.lesson_learned: Do not construct Redis-dependent objects at import time in route modules.

issues.title: Memory Extraction Too Restrictive - AI Loses Context Between Turns
issues.symptom: AI gives generic answers ignoring previous context; user has to repeat information across turns.
issues.root_cause: should_extract_memory() only triggered extraction when user message matched patterns like 'nhớ', 'remember', 'sai', etc. Normal questions like 'Nói chi tiết hơn được không?' were skipped, so AI lost context.
issues.resolution: Removed should_extract_memory() trigger check. Now extracts memories from every chat turn. Added content deduplication in add_memory() to prevent duplicate entries.
issues.lesson_learned: Context memory should be extracted proactively, not only on explicit recall keywords. Users should not need to say 'nhớ' to be remembered.

issues.title: Cache Layer Gaps for 200+ CCU - Missing LLM Response Cache
issues.symptom: No LLM response caching; every query hits expensive LLM API even for repeated/similar questions. TTLs too short (30min-1h). Semantic threshold too strict (0.02).
issues.root_cause: System only cached RAG context (nodes/sections) but not final LLM response. Cache hit rate very low. Query not normalized reducing exact match hits.
issues.resolution: Added LLMResponseCache (2-layer: exact + semantic). Added query normalization. Fixed semantic threshold 0.02→0.08. Increased TTLs: QueryEmbeddingCache 1h→4h, RagResultCache 30min→4h. Added TTL refresh on semantic cache hit.
issues.lesson_learned: For 200+ CCU, must cache full LLM response to bypass expensive API calls. Query normalization critical for exact match cache hits.

issues.title: Dashboard Stats Show 0 Documents Despite Ready Status
issues.symptom: Admin dashboard shows 0 total and 0 active documents even after successful ingestion.
issues.root_cause: HealthService.get_health_data() was a static method returning only configuration status without querying the DocumentRepository for live counts.
issues.resolution: Updated HealthService to accept DocumentRepository via DI and implemented a get_counts() method in the repository to fetch real-time stats.
issues.lesson_learned: Aggregated metrics for system overview must be explicitly integrated with their respective repositories.

issues.title: Document Detail 'Load More' Button Stuck/Silent Failure
issues.symptom: Clicking 'Load more' in document details fetches no new data, and total count (e.g., 54) doesn't match displayed nodes.
issues.root_cause: TreeService.get_document_tree() paginated on root-level sections only, but the total count returned was for ALL sections. If children were expanded, the offset became out of sync with root nodes.
issues.resolution: Refactored TreeService to return a flat paginated list of all sections for the table view, ensuring total_nodes and displayed items are consistent.
issues.lesson_learned: Ensure pagination logic matches the flat or hierarchical nature of the UI component consuming the data.

issues.title: Celery Worker Crash — AMD ROCm stdout Pollution Corrupts Concurrency Value
issues.symptom: workers-1 container restart-loops with: `Error: Invalid value for '-c' / '--concurrency': '/opt/rocm/lib/libamd_smi.so:\nUnable\n3' is not a valid integer.`
issues.root_cause: entrypoint-worker.sh captured hardware concurrency via `$(python -c '... print(concurrency autoscale)' 2>/dev/null)`. The amdsmi library (AMD ROCm GPU detection) prints warning lines like `/opt/rocm/lib/libamd_smi.so: Unable to...` to STDOUT (not stderr), bypassing the 2>/dev/null redirect. These lines were prepended to the actual integer output, making CONCURRENCY an invalid multi-line string passed to `celery worker -c`.
issues.resolution: Added `| tail -n 1` after each Python hardware-detect call to capture only the last printed line (the actual integer). Added regex validation (`^[1-9][0-9]*$`) on CONCURRENCY and AUTOSCALE_MAX with safe fallback values (2/4). Same pattern already existed in entrypoint-api.sh for WORKERS.
issues.lesson_learned: Never trust subprocess stdout to be clean when third-party C libraries (GPU drivers, BLAS, etc.) are imported. Always pipe through `tail -n 1` and validate with regex before using as a numeric argument.

issues.title: LlamaParse API 'agentic' Tier Rejection
issues.symptom: Ingestion task fails with error code 400: MISSING_VERSION_FOR_TIER when 'tier' is set to 'agentic'.
issues.root_cause: The LlamaCloud API key or plan used does not support the 'agentic' tier, or the API endpoint lacks the necessary version metadata for it.
issues.resolution: Removed `tier="agentic"` from the LlamaParse initialization in `llamaparse_adapter.py`, falling back to the default parsing tier.
issues.lesson_learned: Always verify cloud API tier access levels before hardcoding premium/advanced flags in production ingestion pipelines.

issues.title: Double SentenceSplitter — Section Chunking Applied Twice
issues.symptom: Chunks too small (~400 chars), broken context boundaries. OCR header/footer noise creating false section splits.
issues.root_cause: parse_from_markdown() applied SentenceSplitter independently, then the LlamaIndex IngestionPipeline applied another SentenceSplitter on top. Result: double-split context fragmentation.
issues.resolution: Ensured single chunking pass: IngestionPipeline is the only place SentenceSplitter runs. parse_from_markdown() returns each heading section as one node without splitting.
issues.lesson_learned: Chunking should happen exactly once. When piping data through multiple processing stages, verify each stage's transformations don't overlap.
